key_insight: TOPD distills reasoning into a masked diffusion LLM by training on the model’s own denoising traces with a token-level Reverse-KL loss, avoiding reward modeling.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical post-training path for diffusion-based language models that preserves dense supervision while improving reasoning efficiency and alignment with on-policy states.
method: Sample on-policy diffusion trajectories from the target dLLM.
Query a teacher model on the corresponding partially denoised states.
Update the target with token-level Reverse-KL to match teacher distributions along trace-aligned decisions.
evidence_signal: On MATH500, TOPD lets SDAR-4B-Chat match TraDo-4B-Instruct, improving by +5.7 static and +4.5 dynamic accuracy while using 4× fewer rollout rounds and an estimated 96.0× model-compute speedup.
watch_item: The approach still depends on a strong teacher and careful handling of partially denoised states, so gains may be sensitive to teacher quality and trajectory mismatch.
paper_title: Trace-Based On-Policy Distillation for Masked Diffusion Language Models
